video:video.video:title: TWICE reflect on a decade as a group: 'There’s so much more we can do' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Speaking backstage at the Kia Forum during a stop on their 'THIS IS FOR' World Tour, TWICE sit down with Associated Press entertainment journalist Liam McEwan to reflect on a decade together — and the journey still unfolding.

video:video.video:title: Normal life continues in Jerusalem under missile threat as war expands
video:video.video:description: People are seen going about daily chores and children seen playing in playgrounds, signs of normal life under missile threat in Jerusalem, on Tuesday, March 3, 2026.

video:video.video:title: Jerusalem residents run toward shelters as sirens blare
video:video.video:description: People are seen running towards shelters as sirens blare out announcing an incoming attack in Jerusalem on Tuesday, March 3, 2026. (AP Video/Julia Frankel)

video:video.video:title: European couple stranded with their children
video:video.video:description: Viktoriia Lokhmatova, who lives in Serbia, and Michael Crepin, who lives in Belgium, had traveled to Dubai with their children for a special milestone.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong expat recounts search for safety
video:video.video:description: Agnes Chen Pun, a Hong Kong expatriate who moved to Dubai with her husband and their 1 1/2-year-old last year, said she has tried to keep her family safe amid escalating tensions in the United Arab Emirates.

video:video.video:title: US tourists stuck in Dubai
video:video.video:description: Louise Herrle, a retiree from Pittsburgh, and her husband got stranded in Dubai, where the couple had been vacationing when the war broke out.

video:video.video:title: Midflight turnaround impacts tech entrepreneur
video:video.video:description: Two hours into his flight over the weekend from the Qatari capital of Doha to Barcelona, Varun Krishnan says it became clear the aircraft was no longer heading toward Spain.

video:video.video:title: Jafar Panahi plans return to Iran after Oscars | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Jafar Panahi still plans to return home after the Oscars on March 15. The Iranian filmmaker heads to the Academy Awards with two nominations for “It Was Just An Accident,” in the international film and screenplay categories.

video:video.video:title: Academy Awards 2026: 'Sinners' 11-month journey to the Oscars
video:video.video:description: Eleven months after its release, Ryan Coogler's "Sinners" heads into the Oscars on March 15 with a record-breaking 16 nominations.

video:video.video:title: Expect price increases on a range of goods the longer the war in the Middle East lasts
video:video.video:description: The Iran war has effectively halted oil tanker movement in the Strait of Hormuz. It's also disrupting the global supply chain, affecting everything from pharmaceuticals from India, semiconductors from Asia and oil-derived products like fertilizers

video:video.video:title: Sandra Hüller on singing Harry Styles for a 'Project Hail Mary' karaoke scene
video:video.video:description: Sandra Hüller got her daughter's approval to sing Harry Styles' "Sign of the Times" during a "Project Hail Mary" karaoke scene. (March 4)

video:video.video:title: Several casualties reported in Iranian strike on Israel
video:video.video:description: Israel’s paramedic service Magen David Adom, or MDA, said they were providing medical treatment to several casualties, including a 10-year-old girl who was seriously injured in the Beit Shemesh region on Sunday, following rocket salvos from Iran.

video:video.video:title: Photos show an emotional Savannah Guthrie visiting 'Today' show set
video:video.video:description: Savannah Guthrie made an off-camera appearance at offices of NBC's “Today” show, her first time back since her mother Nancy went missing from her Arizona home.

video:video.video:title: Apparent interceptions seen over Qatar's capital Doha
video:video.video:description: An explosion sounded Thursday and was followed by what appeared to be interceptions in the sky, an Associated Press journalist in Doha, Qatar's capital, witnessed.

video:video.video:title: UN sees growing humanitarian crisis in Middle East as conflicts spread
video:video.video:description: The United Nations says the humanitarian situation in the Middle East is a moment of grave peril, with crises escalating rapidly and consequences that are out of control .

video:video.video:title: Palestinians in Gaza are closely following developments as Iran war engulfs the Middle East
video:video.video:description: In a tent camp in Gaza’s southern area of Khan Younis, displaced Palestinians expressed distress at the unfolding regional conflict in the Middle East.

video:video.video:description: Spiraling tensions in the Middle East have rattled some consumers in France on Tuesday as they feared rising oil prices and possible shortages.

video:video.video:description: A blackout has left millions of people without power in Havana and the rest of western Cuba in the latest outage on an island struggling with dwindling oil reserves and a crumbling electric grid.

video:video.video:description: Israeli forces are reinforcing communities along the northern border as missiles and drones continue to be launched from Lebanon, according to The Associated Press's Sam Mednick.

video:video.video:title: Britney Spears arrested, though charge is not clear
video:video.video:description: Britney Spears was arrested Wednesday night in Southern California and booked early the following morning, though the charge was not clear, according to the Ventura County Sheriff’s office website.

video:video.video:description: Foreign Minister Anita Anand announced Wednesday that Canada is arranging evacuation options for Canadians wanting to leave the Middle East.

video:video.video:description: A Chinese company has developed what it claims is the world’s largest flying electrical vertical take-off and landing vehicle, or eVTOL.

video:video.video:description: State Rep. James Talarico defeated Congresswoman Jasmine Crockett in a close race for the Democratic nomination for the U.S.

video:video.video:description: Israeli airstrikes continued to hit the Lebanese capital Tuesday morning. In footage captured by The Associated Press, explosions and smoke plumes were seen rising over the city's skyline.

video:video.video:title: Cat rescued after stuck in tree for 3 days
video:video.video:description: After three days of cries for help, a stranded cat was finally rescued. Video shows professional climber Steven Murrow scaling a tall tree Sunday to reach the feline.

video:video.video:title: Could it be a Rembrandt ? Yes it is ! museum tells owner of newly identified Rembrandt masterpiece
video:video.video:description: A painting that was once rejected as a work by Rembrandt van Rijn has now been acknowledged as a work by the Dutch master, thanks to two years of scrutiny in the city where the then-27-year-old artist painted it in 1633.

video:video.video:description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian apologized on Saturday for Iran's attacks on neighboring countries, suggesting it was the result of miscommunication in the ranks and insisted that it would stop from now on.

video:video.video:title: Cuba's president signed a condolence book for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at the Iranian embassy in Havana
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel visited the Iranian embassy in Havana on Thursday and signed a condolences book for the Supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei who was killed in U.S-Iranian airstrikes in the country.

video:video.video:description: A Cleveland judge set bail at $2 million Friday for a woman accused of killing her two young daughters and burying them in suitcases in shallow graves.

video:video.video:description: Senators on Capitol Hill reacting Noem firing after mounting criticism and Mullin being named as her replacement Trump made the announcement Thursday.

video:video.video:description: Hundreds of people released sky lanterns in New Taipei to mark the end of Lunar New Year, writing wishes for the year ahead.

video:video.video:description: Plumes of smoke were seen rising across various parts of Tehran on Thursday morning as U.S.-Israeli airstrikes continued to pound the Iranian capital.

video:video.video:title: Quinta Brunson and Bowen Yang honored at 2026 GLAAD Awards
video:video.video:description: Quinta Brunson, Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers were honored at the 2026 GLAAD Awards with Brunson receiving the GLAAD Vanguard Award and Yang and Rogers the Stephen F.

video:video.video:title: U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran threaten to worsen inflation and slow the economy
video:video.video:description: Oil prices jumped Tuesday for the second straight day and gas prices moved higher in the United States, underscoring the threat of rising inflation posed by the Iran war.

video:video.video:description: Azerbaijan on Thursday accused Iran of a drone attack on its territory that injured four civilians, and it vowed to retaliate in a sign that the war in the Middle East was spreading.

video:video.video:description: An Italian tourist stranded in Dubai due to the US-Israeli strikes on Iran and Tehran's subsequent retaliation, left the UAE on a bus bound for Oman on Tuesday, organized by the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

video:video.video:title: Travellers from Dubai reunite with family and friends as they arrive in Lisbon on commerical flight
video:video.video:description: The first flight departing from Dubai to Lisbon since the beginning of the Iran war arrived on Wednesday night carrying around 300 passengers.

video:video.video:description: Israeli ambassador Danny Danon told reporters at the United Nations "we will be able to show that superiority in the next few days" and he reported that they have control of almost all of Iran's airspace.

video:video.video:description: Authorities in Cleveland are working to identify two young half-sisters whose bodies were found buried inside suitcases on the city’s east side.

video:video.video:description: With no sign of the conflict abating anytime soon, President Donald Trump says that the military operations are likely to last four to five weeks but that he was prepared “to go far longer than that.”

video:video.video:description: Authorities say Britney Spears was pulled over, after reports that her BMW was driving fast and erratically, on a highway Wednesday night and jailed after taking a series of field sobriety tests.

video:video.video:description: Israel sent troops into southern Lebanon on Tuesday and warned residents of more than 80 villages to evacuate as the Iran-backed militant Hezbollah group said it was ready for an “open war” with Israel in the wake of the U.S.

video:video.video:description: U.S. Under Secretary of Defense for Policy Elbridge Colby said Wednesday that the U.S. and Israel have "overlapping" objectives for the war in Iran, with the U.S. military focusing on degrading and destroying Iran's ability to project military power.

video:video.video:title: Iranian president says new leadership council ‘has begun its work’ after death of supreme leader
video:video.video:description: Pezeshkian made the comment in a prerecorded message aired on Iranian state television. He is one of three officials on the council.

video:video.video:description: Israel renewed its assault on southern Lebanon early Sunday, including targeting commanders of the Lebanese branch of Iran’s Revolutionary Guards’ Quds Force, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised “many surprises” for the next phase of the conflict.

video:video.video:description: Fake images spread online as conflict in Middle East widens

video:video.video:description: Some British tourists who were stranded in the United Arab Emirates were relieved to land safely in London's Heathrow Airport on Monday night.

video:video.video:description: Speaking at U.S. Central Command, which oversees the Middle East, Hegseth said the U.S. is solely controlling the timeline of activities in Iran and will do so “as long as it takes to ensure the United States of America achieves” its objectives.

video:video.video:description: Arkansas voters will choose nominees for a full slate of federal, state and local offices in primary elections Tuesday. (AP Video shot by Adrian Sainz)

video:video.video:description: Sailors aboard the Iranian warship that was struck by a U.S.-fired torpedo off the coast of Sri Lanka were being treated in hospital on Thursday.

video:video.video:description: Israel carried out airstrikes targeting Iran’s presidential office and facilities linked to the Supreme Leader on Tuesday.

video:video.video:description: Iranian missiles could be seen being intercepted above Doha on Saturday night into Sunday morning.

video:video.video:description: Global energy trade is in turmoil as war around the Persian Gulf chokes off oil and natural gas shipments, causing prices to soar.

video:video.video:description: The intensity of the attacks, the killing of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and the lack of any apparent exit plan indicated the conflict would not end anytime soon.

video:video.video:description: Greenland’s fishermen are struggling as warming weather from climate change makes the sea ice unreliable and the fish harder to predict.

video:video.video:description: Millions of people in India celebrated the colorful Holi festival on Wednesday. Thousands danced and got soaked in colored water and powder at the Swaminarayan Temple, reveling in traditional Gujarati music.

video:video.video:description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian also apologized on Saturday for Iran's attacks on neighbouring countries, suggesting it was the result of miscommunication in the ranks and insisted that it would stop from now on.

video:video.video:description: Iran hit the U.S. Embassy in Saudi Arabia’s capital with a drone strike early Tuesday as it kept striking targets around the region, while the United States and Israel pounded Iran with airstrikes.

video:video.video:description: Lioness Chloe Kelly becomes the first soccer player to have a Barbie doll made in her likeness, as part of the brand's Dream Team collection celebrating women. (March 5)

video:video.video:description: The Oscar-nominated documentary short, "The Devil is Busy," focuses on a day in the life of a women's health clinic in Georgia, where women's reproductive rights have been strictly limited in the U.S.

video:video.video:description: Airstrikes damaged a Tehran hospital and nearby homes, shattering windows and sending debris into apartments.

video:video.video:title: Joy and relief as Ukrainian prisoners of war meet families after long months in Russian captivity
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian prisoners of war were warmly greeted by their loved ones on Thursday after months of captivity, as Russia and Ukraine carried out an exchange of 200 prisoners each.

video:video.video:title: Dove Cameron talks differences between Disney and '56 days'
video:video.video:description: Dove Cameron says acting in "thriller, dark, drama" shows comes naturally. She stars in the new Amazon Prime series "56 Days."

video:video.video:description: A commercial airplane was seen taking off amid smoke and explosions after Israeli airstrikes hit the Lebanese capital Tuesday morning. (AP video: Hussein Al-Sanea)

video:video.video:description: Worshipers attended a rally after Friday prayers in Tehran, as the Iranian capital continues to be pounded by airstrikes, and Iran said it had fired ballistic missiles toward Israel.

video:video.video:title: 'This had to be done': Trump claims to have done the world a 'favor' with attacks on Iran
video:video.video:description: It comes as Iran's president apologized on Saturday for attacks on “neighboring countries” even as its missiles and drones flew toward Gulf Arab states and Trump threatened that the country would be “hit very hard.”

video:video.video:description: Japanese baseball star Shohei Ohtani thrilled a packed crowd on Friday, launching ball after ball over the fence during batting practice before hitting a grand slam at the World Baseball Classic.

video:video.video:description: Swaths of Cuba remained without power on Thursday, nearly a day after a huge blackout hit the western part of the island in the latest outage blamed on a fragile electric grid and a lack of fuel.

video:video.video:description: A fire broke out in an oil industrial facility in Fujairah, one of the seven emirates of the United Arab Emirates, as forces intercepted an Iranian drone attack, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: What does China’s response to the Iran attack say about its foreign policy?
video:video.video:description: China said it was “highly concerned” about the Iran attack — but stayed out of the fight. Here’s what that response signals about its foreign policy.

video:video.video:title: John Cornyn warns Ken Paxton would hurt GOP in Texas runoff
video:video.video:description: Sen. John Cornyn warns Texas Republicans that rival Ken Paxton would be “dead weight at the top of the ticket” in November general election, jeopardizing President Donald Trump's agenda.

video:video.video:description: Chinese electronics maker ZTE has launched a new “AI species” at Barcelona mobile fair MWC. The furry “iMoochi” is designed to be an emotional companion and uses artificial intelligence to develop an evolving personality.

video:video.video:description: Under Secretary of Defense Elbridge Colby facing questions on Capitol Hill about the U.S. involvement in Iran war and if there is an exit plan.

video:video.video:description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan said Turkey is issuing "warnings" to prevent missile threats, after NATO defenses on Wednesday intercepted an Iranian ballistic missile heading toward its airspace.

video:video.video:description: Crude oil prices are rising sharply as markets react to the war between Israel and Iran, fuelling concerns about energy costs across Europe. Here's what it means.

video:video.video:title: Iran's succession: what happens now?

video:video.video:title: Mourners take to streets of Tehran following confirmation of Khamenei's death
video:video.video:description: Khamenei was killed in a major attack on Iran launched by Israel and the United States, throwing the future of the Islamic Republic into doubt and raising the risk of regional instability.

video:video.video:title: Minnesota's governor and attorney general appear before House committee investigating fraud
video:video.video:description: Tim Walz and Keith Ellison were called to testify before the panel exploring fraud and misuse of government funds in the state.

video:video.video:title: Trump denies Israel forced his hand on Iran strike timing and says he thought Iran would 'attack first'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump disputes the idea that Israel forced timing of the Iran strikes. “If anything, I might have forced Israel’s hand," he says.

video:video.video:title: Texas residents share mixed views on US-Israeli strikes in Iran
video:video.video:description: A day before Texas voters choose their nominees in a key U.S. Senate race, they're reacting to the U.S. military attacks on Iran (AP Video: Kendria LaFleur and Juan Lozano)

video:video.video:title: Hegseth speaking at U.S. Central Command touts 'total dominance' in skies and seas
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is visiting MacDill Air Force Base in Florida and held meetings with Adm. Brad Cooper, who leads U.S. Central Command.

video:video.video:title: Israelis mark holiday of Purim in fortified bomb shelters
video:video.video:description: The holiday of Purim is usually marked in Israel with boisterous street parades and costume parties to celebrate the Jewish victory over an ancient Persian ruler.

video:video.video:title: Three US fighter jets mistakenly downed by Kuwaiti fire during Iranian air assault
video:video.video:description: Three American fighter jets were mistakenly downed by friendly Kuwaiti fire during an Iranian air assault, the U.S. military said.

video:video.video:title: Sri Lanka navy releases video showing rescue of Iranian sailors from warship off coast
video:video.video:description: Sri Lanka has released video showing its navy personnel rescuing Iranian sailors from a warship that was sunk by a torpedo from a U.S. submarine on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth won't rule out ground troops in Iran, insists conflict won't be 'endless'
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth wouldn't rule out U.S. troops on the ground in Iran, but declared the United States would not be dragged into an “endless” war.

video:video.video:title: FBI investigates Texas bar shooting that killed 2 and wounded 14 as possible terrorist act
video:video.video:description: Authorities say a gunman wearing clothes with an Iranian flag design and the words “Property of Allah” killed two people and wounded 14 at a Texas bar.

video:video.video:title: Israelis react after Iranian strikes on Beit Shemesh kills several
video:video.video:description: First responders look for survivors Sunday, hours after a missile struck a synagogue in a central Israeli town.

video:video.video:title: 'President Trump has all the latitude in the world,' Hegseth insists as Iran conflict intensifies
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth dismisses questions about the time frame of the widening conflict in Iran, saying: “President Trump has all the latitude in the world to talk about how long it may or may not take.

video:video.video:title: Israeli tanks move to Lebanon border as fighting escalates
video:video.video:description: Israeli tanks were seen being transferred to the border with Lebanon on Thursday as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah escalated amid the wider Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Demonstrators in New York march in support of US military operation in Iran
video:video.video:description: People demonstrated in New York on Sunday in support of U.S. strikes in Iran and leadership change in the country.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: Iran pummeled by airstrikes as it launches new wave of attacks around the region
video:video.video:description: Azerbaijan on Thursday accused Iran of a drone attack on its territory, and it vowed to retaliate in a sign that the war in the Middle East was spreading.

video:video.video:title: House narrowly rejects Iran war powers resolution
video:video.video:description: The House narrowly rejected a war powers resolution that would halt President Donald Trump’s attack on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong investors remain cautious amid escalation in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: Asian shares have swooned, with South Korea's benchmark plunging 10%, after a global sell-off for stocks hit Wall Street. Oil prices climbed still higher.

video:video.video:title: Spring in India: Colourful powder fills the air as an early Holi festival begins
video:video.video:description: Hundreds gathered in the northern Indian city of Mathura at a temple believed to be the birthplace of Lord Krishna, one of the most revered Hindu gods.

video:video.video:title: Taiwan's sky lantern festival visitors hope for more peaceful future
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of people released lanterns into Taiwan’s night sky at an annual festival in New Taipei on Tuesday evening. (AP video shot by: Taijing Wu)

video:video.video:title: Tourists’ sweat creates a salty film coating Michelangelo’s ’The Last Judgment’
video:video.video:description: Michelangelo’s “The Last Judgement” is getting a facial, with restorers removing a chalky white film of salt that has accumulated over the Renaissance masterpiece since its last major renovation three decades ago.

video:video.video:title: Winter Paralympics begins for its 50th anniversary
video:video.video:description: The Winter Paralympics are in Milan Cortina to celebrate their 50th anniversary, with China looking to extend its dominance as a Paralympic powerhouse and Ukraine and other nations boycotting the opening ceremony over the return of the Russian flag and anthem.

video:video.video:title: Israeli warplanes bomb Tehran
video:video.video:description: Israeli warplanes bombed Tehran early on Friday and again on Saturday. Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump appeared to rule out talks with Iran in a social media post calling for its “unconditional surrender.”

video:video.video:title: BLACKPINK's Jisoo teases solo album
video:video.video:description: BLACKPINK’s Jisoo says the band’s collaboration with Coldplay’s Chris Martin brought “fresh” energy to their latest album.

video:video.video:title: Israeli warplanes bomb Tehran as war in the region escalates
video:video.video:description: Israeli warplanes bombed Tehran early on Friday while U.S. President appeared to rule out talks with Iran in a social media post calling for its “unconditional surrender.”

video:video.video:title: Israelis living near border face renewed Israel–Hezbollah clashes
video:video.video:description: Lebanon was dragged into the war in the Middle East early Monday, which erupted following U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Gunman was not on the FBI's radar before he opened fire on a crowded Texas bar, authorities say
video:video.video:description: The man who opened fire outside a crowded Texas bar and killed two people in an attack that wounded 14 others was not on the radar of authorities before the shooting, federal and local investigators said Monday.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong and Japan markets react to attack on Iran
video:video.video:description: Asia markets in Hong Kong and Japan react after the U.S. and Israel's attacks on Iran.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 6
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, March 6: More strikes in Iran; Iran fires missiles and drones at Persian Gulf nations; Americans stranded in Middle East begin returning to U.S.; Trump fires Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem.

video:video.video:title: Nightclub bombing in Peru injures 33, including minors, authorities say
video:video.video:description: A bombing at a nightclub in Peru has injured 33 people, including minors, authorities said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: New exhibit spotlights Japanese-American soldiers who fought in World War II
video:video.video:description: A new traveling exhibit tells the story of Japanese American soldiers who fought in World War II, even as their families were incarcerated as enemies of the state.

video:video.video:title: Israel reinforces northern border with Lebanon but has yet to evacuate villages
video:video.video:description: Israeli forces reinforce northern border communities as missiles and drones launch from Lebanon, AP’s Sam Mednick reports.

video:video.video:title: At least 7 killed in Ukraine's Kharkiv after Russian missile hits apartment building
video:video.video:description: Russia has fired tens of thousands of Iranian-designed Shahed drones at Ukraine since it invaded its neighbor just over four years ago.

video:video.video:title: Democratic senators comment on the firing of Kristi Noem
video:video.video:description: Democrat senators on Capitol Hill react to President Donald Trump's firing of his embattled Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem. (AP video: Rick Gentilo)

video:video.video:title: Displaced families in Lebanon seek refuge after Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: Displaced families from southern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs sought refuge on Monday in schools in the capital after Israel launched its deadliest barrage of airstrikes in more than a year, striking hours after Hezbollah fired missiles across the border.

video:video.video:title: Thousands demonstrate in Los Angeles to support Iranians
video:video.video:description: A few thousand demonstrators gathered on Sunday in Los Angeles. Many people marching waved the flag of Iran, before the Islamic Revolution, during the afternoon rally on the city’s west side.

video:video.video:description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem defended her agency in testimony and under questioning at a Senate hearing on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Melania Trump presides over a UN Security Council meeting
video:video.video:description: The U.S. first lady presided over a U.N. Security Council meeting on Monday titled “Children, Technology, and Education in Conflict.”

video:video.video:title: Texas bar shooting leaves 3 dead and 14 wounded as FBI investigates possible terrorism
video:video.video:description: Police say officers in Austin shot and killed the gunman in the attack. The FBI says the shooting is being investigated as a potential act of terrorism.

video:video.video:title: US markets drop sharply on fears of widening war
video:video.video:description: A worldwide sell-off for stocks is slamming onto Wall Street, and oil prices are leaping even higher as worries rise that the war with Iran is widening.

video:video.video:title: Lionel Messi and Inter Miami feted by President Donald Trump at the White House for MLS Cup title
video:video.video:description: Lionel Messi and his Inter Miami teammates were honored by President Donald Trump at the White House on Thursday for winning last year's MLS Cup.

video:video.video:title: UN condemns Iran attacks, call for a 'return to the table and return to diplomacy'
video:video.video:description: The United Nations is continuing to condemn the war in the Middle East as the conflict spiraled further Monday with Israel and the U.S. continuing to pound Iran. U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 7
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, March 7th: Trump encourages Latin American leaders to use military action against cartels; Iranian President apologizes to Gulf Arab states: Oil and gas prices rapidly rise as Iran war continues; Severe weather kill at least six in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: UN's Secretary-General hopes 'for a swift end to the conflict' in Iran
video:video.video:description: “The Secretary-General continues to be very concerned about the situation in Lebanon where the Lebanese people are once again in the crosshairs of conflict," the spokesperson for the Secretary told the press.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 6
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, March 6th: Evidence suggests deadly airstrike on Iranian school likely from U.S.; Donald Trump wants "unconditional surrender" of Iran; U.S. unemployment rate rises to 4.4% and former presidents attend Rev.

video:video.video:title: Kristi Noem out as Homeland Security secretary
video:video.video:description: Noem’s departure caps a tumultuous tenure overseeing immigration enforcement tactics that have been met with protests and lawsuits, as well as a wave of criticism over the response to deadly Texas floods.

video:video.video:title: Trump awards Medal of Honor to US Army service members
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump awarded the Medal of Honor to three U.S. Army soldiers at the White House on Monday, celebrating heroes of old wars as he defended his launch of a new one.

video:video.video:title: Israeli military says video shows strike on Khamenei's bunker in Tehran days after his death
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military released a video that it said showed a strike on a bunker that allegedly belonged to Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei on Friday, days after his death.

video:video.video:title: UN chief warns that the war in the Middle East 'could spiral beyond anyone's control'
video:video.video:description: U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres called for an end to the war in the Middle East that now affects more than a dozen countries, and for serious diplomatic negotiations, warning the conflict could spiral out of control.

video:video.video:title: Iranian woman in Indiana views US, Israel attacks as help but says Iranians must determine country’s future
video:video.video:description: Afagh Farhadi, a postdoctoral researcher from Iran who has been in the U.S. since 2017, says she is hopeful about the future of her home country but wants Iranians to control their own destiny.

video:video.video:title: Iran's World Cup place in US put in doubt by Middle East war - AP explains
video:video.video:description: Iran’s place at the men’s World Cup in three months’ time was put in doubt Monday amid an escalating Middle East conflict sparked by the soccer tournament’s co-host the United States.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth says an American submarine sank an Iranian warship with a torpedo in international waters
video:video.video:description: The U.S. sank an Iranian warship in international waters, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Wednesday, as it intensified its bombardment with Israel of Iran's security forces and other symbols of power.

video:video.video:title: A chance to own Kurt Cobain's 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' guitar or John Lennon's piano
video:video.video:description: Christie's auction house in New York is set to have one of the most extensive Rock n Roll and pop culture auctions in recent history next week.

video:video.video:title: For survivors, Rhode Island clergy abuse report brings vindication
video:video.video:description: A report by Rhode Island’s attorney general detailing decades of sexual abuse by Catholic clergy is giving survivors a sense of vindication while renewing calls for accountability and support.

video:video.video:title: J Balvin jumps at chance to record FIFA World Cup anthem
video:video.video:description: J Balvin helped reimagine Van Halen’s "Jump" for Coca-Cola’s official anthem for the FIFA World Cup 2026.

video:video.video:title: Trump hosts White House roundtable aimed at 'saving college sports'
video:video.video:description: Last year, President Donald Trump endorsed the the Score Act, which would set new rules for the payment of college athletes and set nationwide standards for compensation, but the act has stalled in Congress.

video:video.video:title: Sri Lanka recovers 87 bodies from Iranian warship sunk off its coast by a US submarine
video:video.video:description: A torpedo fired by a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship off the coast of Sri Lanka, whose navy said Wednesday it recovered 87 bodies and rescued 32 people.

video:video.video:title: Footage captures a powerful tornado in Michigan devastating homes in its path
video:video.video:description: At Lisa Piper's home near Union City, Michigan, she can be heard repeatedly yelling out, as she films from her back deck a ferocious rotating column of air that appears to be a tornado tear through a section of buildings across the lake from her.

video:video.video:title: 'Outlander' cast says goodbye in New York
video:video.video:description: After more than a decade of time travel, war, romance and revolution, the stars of “Outlander” launch the show's eighth and final season at a New York premiere.

video:video.video:title: Liberia's largest gold miner spilled dangerous chemicals, records show
video:video.video:description: For generations, families in Jikandor village fished and drank water from a river that runs through Liberia’s dense rainforest.

video:video.video:title: Jobs report reveals unexpected 92,000 job cuts as unemployment rises
video:video.video:description: U.S. employers cut 92,000 jobs last month and pushed unemployment up to 4.4% in what economist Diane Swonk calls a “jobless boom.” (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: Iran war raises worries in the US over higher gas prices
video:video.video:description: Oil surged higher and showed no signs of halting its rapid climb a week after the U.S. and Israel launched major attacks on Iran that escalated into a war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Viola Davis talks Black maternal health in 'The Ebony Canal'
video:video.video:description: Viola Davis talks about why she's supporting "The Ebony Canal," a documentary film she narrated that examines Black maternal health, and disparities in infant mortality.

video:video.video:title: Raves rise in Lagos as young Nigerians seek alternative party scene
video:video.video:description: For decades Nigerian clubs revolved around “table culture”— a system separating high-spending guests from others and reinforcing social status.

video:video.video:title: An inside look at an AP reporter's missile bunker in Jerusalem
video:video.video:description: AP reporter Julia Frankel saw people running toward shelters in Jerusalem while sirens blared out announcing an incoming attack on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Iran confirms death of 86-year-old Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
video:video.video:description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who assembled theocratic power in Iran over the decades as its supreme leader and sought to turn it into a regional powerhouse, bringing it into confrontation with Israel and the United States over its nuclear program while crushing democracy protesters at home, has died.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 2
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, March 2nd: Trump says Iran war may last more than 4 or 5 weeks, Hegseth briefs reporters on war aims; Global energy markets spike; FBI investigates whether Texas bar shooting was an act of terrorism.

video:video.video:title: Austin police release bodycam videos of officers locating shooting suspect
video:video.video:description: The 53 year old suspect, who was killed by police, is seen holding an AR-15 rifle on a video captured by a nearby parked vehicle.

video:video.video:title: Michelle Williams wins Actor Award
video:video.video:description: Michelle Williams says the "Dying for Sex" cast will "always be connected." She won the Actor Award for her lead role in the Hulu and Disney+ limited series.

video:video.video:title: Millions left without power after major blackout hits Cuba's western region
video:video.video:description: A blackout has hit the western half of Cuba. Millions of people in Havana and beyond were without power on Wednesday in the latest outage to affect an island struggling with dwindling oil reserves and a crumbling electric grid.

video:video.video:title: US-Israeli bombardment of Iran intensifies, as Tehran presses attacks across the region
video:video.video:description: Israel stepped up attacks on Iranian missile launchers and weapons factories, while Iran fired dozens of ballistic missiles at Israel and launched drone attacks across the Gulf.

video:video.video:description: Lebanese civilians have been fleeing areas targeted by Israel, that carried out dozens of airstrikes throughout the day, hitting the capital's southern suburbs as well as areas in southern and eastern Lebanon.

video:video.video:description: Amir Saeid Iravani on Monday said the United States was guilty of war crimes against his nation. Addressing reporters in New York, Iravani accused the United States of hypocrisy.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the "worst case" scenario in Iran would be if the nation's next leader is "as bad" as the late Ayatollah Khamenei.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV said Sunday he was “profoundly concerned” about the U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran and urged both sides to “stop the spiral of violence before it becomes an irreparable abyss.”

video:video.video:title: Witness video shows chaotic moments of Texas bar shooting
video:video.video:description: A gunman wearing clothes with an Iranian flag design and the words “Property of Allah” killed two people and wounded 14 early Sunday at a Texas bar, a law enforcement official told The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Baby Sumatra tiger making good progress at Berlin zoo
video:video.video:description: A Sumatran tiger cub born at Berlin Zoo in early January successfully passed its first veterinary examination on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Sheryl Lee Ralph: 'I'm blessed beyond compare'
video:video.video:description: Sheryl Lee Ralph says she is "blessed beyond compare" after she was named Time Magazine Woman of the Year.

video:video.video:title: Eddie Vedder says more Pearl Jam music is on the way
video:video.video:description: Eddie Vedder says that after 35 years, Pearl Jam is "more of a group now than ever" and fans can expect new music.

video:video.video:title: US envoy Waltz defends attack on Iran at emergency Security Council meeting
video:video.video:description: His remarks come as the United Nations chief Antonio Guterres condemned the U.S.-Israeli airstrikes on Iran and called for an immediate return to negotiations “to pull the region, and our world, back from the brink.”

video:video.video:title: Protesters clash with anti-riot police near US embassy in Baghdad over killing of Khamenei
video:video.video:description: Journalists of The Associated Press saw dozens of people attempting to cross a bridge leading to Baghdad’s heavily fortified Green Zone, which houses Iraqi government buildings and the sprawling U.S.

video:video.video:description: Droves of young people are turning to tactile, analog hobbies and activities, such as needlework and mahjong, as a means of escaping technology and reconnecting with creativity and exploration.

video:video.video:title: Canada and Australia leaders urge war de-escalation, but agree Iran can't get nuclear weapons
video:video.video:description: Canadian and Australian prime ministers have called for a de-escalation of the Iran war but added the Iranians must never gain a nuclear weapon.

video:video.video:title: Decades of abuse by Catholic priests in Rhode Island revealed in sprawling investigation
video:video.video:description: A new report says Rhode Island Catholic clergy abused hundreds of children for decades, while church leaders worked to downplay and minimize the scandal.

video:video.video:title: Apple’s 'big week' starts with a pair of $599 devices aimed at budget buyers
video:video.video:description: Apple’s new budget-friendly iPhone 17e, a new entry-level Macbook Neo, updated iPad Air models, refreshed monitors and higher-end chipsets were all on display at a hands-on media event in New York.

video:video.video:title: Key moments from Bill Clinton's testimony on Jeffrey Epstein
video:video.video:description: Videos of former President Bill Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton answering questions about convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein have been released by a House committee investigating the late financier.

video:video.video:title: Triceratops skeleton 'Trey' is headed to auction in a hot market
video:video.video:description: A triceratops skeleton that once greeted visitors at a Wyoming museum is heading to an online auction as dinosaur prices keep soaring.

video:video.video:title: Major historical documents arrive in Kansas City as part of nation's 250th anniversary celebration
video:video.video:description: Some of the United States' most important historical documents arrived in Kansas City Monday as part of the country's 250th anniversary commemoration (AP video by Nick Ingram)

video:video.video:title: Paxton, headed to US Senate primary runoff, says ‘Texas is not for sale’
video:video.video:description: Longtime Sen. John Cornyn and MAGA favorite Ken Paxton are heading to a May runoff in Texas’ Republican Senate primary, setting up what's expected to be a nasty and expensive second round and a renewed push to win the endorsement of President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Breast cancer survivor knits prostheses in Kenya instead of costly silicone
video:video.video:description: A Kenyan woman who survived breast cancer is knitting prostheses and training others to make them in a country where silicone ones are expensive.

video:video.video:description: Former North Carolina Gov. Roy Cooper won the Democratic nomination for U.S. Senate and will face Republican Michael Whatley in the fall for a seat currently held by retiring Sen.

video:video.video:description: Nasdaq Texas is structured as a dual listing venue, meaning companies can list on the Texas-based exchange while remaining part of Nasdaq’s broader national and global marketplace.

video:video.video:description: Iran has not requested talks with the United States to bring an end to the widening war, Iran’s ambassador to Egypt told AP Thursday, denying comments by US President Donald Trump that the country “wants to talk.”

video:video.video:title: Displaced residents of Beirut's southern suburbs comment after Israeli military evacuation notice
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people displaced from Beirut's southern suburbs filled the streets and public squares of the Lebanese capital.

video:video.video:description: The vote is an extraordinary test in Congress for a conflict that has rapidly spread across the Middle East with no clear U.S. exit strategy.

video:video.video:description: "The Bride!" stars Jessie Buckley and Christian Bale join filmmaker Maggie Gyllenhaal at the New York premiere of her re-imagining of “The Bride of Frankenstein.”

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he gives the U.S. a 15 out of 10 grade for the Iran war. The president opened his remarks, at an unrelated White House event on AI, saying that the U.S. is “doing very well on the war front, to put it mildly.”

video:video.video:title: Travel insiders give advice amidst Iran war travel chaos
video:video.video:description: The Iran war continues to upended travel across the Middle East and beyond. video:video.video:title: Strong storms in Oklahoma as millions are under tornado watch
video:video.video:description: A powerful storm in Oklahoma is being investigated as a possible tornado as millions of Americans are under threat from severe weather.

video:video.video:title: AP video shows apparent interceptions in the sky, after the sound of explosion was heard in Doha
video:video.video:description: AP video shows apparent interceptions in the sky, after the sound of an explosion was heard in Doha, Qatar's capital.

video:video.video:description: At the New York premiere of the second season of "Ted," Seth MacFarlane explained why the show used AI to make him look like Bill Clinton for one of the episodes.

video:video.video:description: At least 555 people have been killed in Iran so far by the U.S.-Israeli campaign, the Iranian Red Crescent Society said.

video:video.video:description: Intense Israeli airstrikes pounded the capital of Iran early Friday as the U.S. apparently struck an Iranian drone carrier at sea, intensifying its campaign targeting the Islamic Republic’s fleet of warships.

video:video.video:description: Hundreds of thousands of travelers found themselves stranded in the Middle East after Israel and the United States attacked Iran on Saturday and Iran struck back on Gulf states and Israel.

video:video.video:description: South Carolina residents honor the legacy of civil rights leader Rev. Jesse Jackson as he lies in state. (AP Video: Eric Verduzco)

video:video.video:title: What is behind the fascination with true crime? Inside the world’s largest serial killer exhibit
video:video.video:description: Serial killers have always intrigued audiences, with their atrocious crimes told in documentaries, podcasts, and TV shows.

video:video.video:title: Chloé shows '70s boho reverie
video:video.video:description: Designer Chemena Kamali unveils Chloé's Fall/Winter 2026-2027 Women's collection in Paris, before stars including Oprah Winfrey and Olivia Rodrigo. (Mar. 5)

video:video.video:title: Delroy Lindo reveals his to-do list
video:video.video:description: Oscar-nominee and "Sinners" star Delroy Lindo is inspired by his roots when it comes to projects he wants to do in the future. (March 3)

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, March 5th: Netanyahu visits an Israeli Air Force base; Voting is peaceful in Nepal's nationwide election; Swaths of Cuba remain without power as repairs continue; Princess of Wales visits Indian dance company in Leicester

video:video.video:title: Shakira breaks attendance record with free concert in Mexico
video:video.video:description: Shakira performed a free concert Sunday night in Mexico City that drew more than 400,000 people to the city's main square, setting a new attendance record for the historic plaza known as Zócalo.

video:video.video:title: Stranded French travellers make it back home amid war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: Dozens of French travelers who were stranded in the Middle East landed in Paris on Wednesday as travel across the region remained heavily disrupted by the widening Iran war.

video:video.video:description: A Texas judge on Wednesday ordered Camp Mystic to preserve damaged cabins but stopped short of blocking reopening plans after a family of one of the 25 girls and two counselors who died last summer sued to keep the camp closed.

video:video.video:description: Stranded Americans across the Middle East say they’ve been left to navigate flight cancellations and confusing government guidance on their own.

video:video.video:title: Major historical documents start tour across US as part of nation’s 250th anniversary celebration
video:video.video:description: Some of the United States’ most important historical documents are beginning a first-of-its kind journey as part of the country’s 250th anniversary commemoration.

video:video.video:title: Americans see price hike at gas pump amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: Consumers in Mississippi and Massachusetts paid more at the gas pump Tuesday as fighting in the Middle East sent prices climbing by double digits.

video:video.video:title: Video shows view from across street as shots are fired outside Texas bar
video:video.video:description: A witness video from near the scene of a shooting at a bar in Texas' capital shows people crouched down on the ground as several gunshots were heard and police ran to the bar.

video:video.video:title: AP explains Mojtaba Khamenei as a possible new Iran leader

video:video.video:description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rebounded slightly Thursday after briefly dropping more than 1,000 points when the price of oil spiked to its highest level since the summer of 2024.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead at 86
video:video.video:description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who assembled theocratic power in Iran over the decades as its supreme leader and sought to turn it into a regional powerhouse, bringing it into confrontation with Israel and the United States over its nuclear program while crushing democracy protesters at home, has died, Ira

video:video.video:title: More airstrikes hit Tehran
video:video.video:description: More airstrikes hit Iran's capital of Tehran as night falls on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: 911 calls from immigration detention facility reveal detainees in medical distress
video:video.video:description: The emergency calls from a Texas immigration detention center included repeated suicide attempts by detainees, seizures, injuries from fights and a pregnant woman in pain.

video:video.video:description: Senate Republicans voted down an effort Wednesday to halt the Trump administration's war against Iran, demonstrating early support for a conflict that has rapidly spread across the Middle East with no clear exit strategy.

video:video.video:description: Visitors to the Capitol in Washington now have a visible reminder of the violent attack against the building on Jan. 6, 2021, and the officers who fought and were injured that day.

video:video.video:description: Remains of Iranian drones intercepted by the United States caused damage to buildings and sparked fear among residents in Ankawa, a suburb of Irbil in northern Iraq on Wednesday evening.
